text.title: nodejs-express-fastify Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for nodejs-express-fastify as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Callback-based async handler in Express: User.findById(id, (err, user) => {...}).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Callback-based code cannot use try/catch for error propagation. Errors require explicit res.status(500) calls in every callback, creating inconsistent shapes and missed error paths. Use async/await with next(err) in catch.

failure-mode.description: Async Express handler without try/catch and next(err): async (req, res) => { const u = await find(); res.json(u); }.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Express 4 does not catch rejected promises from async handlers. If find() rejects, the response hangs indefinitely. Wrap in try/catch and call next(err), or use Express 5 / express-async-errors.

failure-mode.description: Route handler without a schema in Fastify or validate() middleware in Express.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unvalidated request bodies reach business logic and databases with arbitrary shapes. Schema validation is the only reliable defense against malformed input.

failure-mode.description: CORS configured with origin: '*' and credentials: true.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This combination is invalid per the CORS spec and causes a browser error at runtime. Browsers block credentialed cross-origin requests when the server responds with a wildcard origin.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoded port, JWT secret, or database URL as string literals in application code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Secrets committed to source or bundled into images are leaked to anyone with repository or container access. Use process.env and validate at startup; reject missing required config.

failure-mode.description: Fastify auth decorator registered in a plain plugin (not wrapped with fastify-plugin).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fastify's encapsulation isolates decorators to the plugin's scope. Routes outside the plugin cannot call app.authenticate. The failure is silent — the decorator simply does not exist on the outer instance.

failure-mode.description: Logging raw req.body or req.headers without redacting authorization, cookie, and password fields.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Production logs containing credentials create audit failures and enable credential theft via log aggregation systems. Use pino's redact option to strip sensitive fields before logging.
